safety_rule: Use sources only to derive broad genre grammar. Never reproduce a recognizable artist, face, copyrighted character, logo, exact costume, signature prop, music-video shot sequence, or exact stage design.

rock.design_basis: Instrument-led physical performance, visible stage mechanics, compressed tension that opens into collective release.
narrative_modes: build from rehearsal-like restraint into a full-stage release
treat feedback, cables, amplifiers, and empty venue architecture as emotional pressure
alternate performer isolation with ensemble impact
environments: working rehearsal room with visible cables, cases, and practical lamps
backstage loading corridor opening toward stage light
compact club stage with layered amplifiers and low ceiling truss
empty arena floor seen from the edge of the stage
industrial sound-check space with movable risers
camera_language: low lateral tracking close to instruments with foreground hardware crossing frame
shoulder-height handheld composition with controlled off-axis urgency
wide stage geography followed by a fast push toward the lead action
rear-stage angle looking through performers toward open audience space
motion_language: body weight, fabric, cables, and instrument straps react to strong beats
short physical camera surges settle into readable held frames
ensemble movement converges on chorus accents without chaotic shaking
edit_language: hard cut on a drum or guitar accent followed by a longer reaction shot
match cut between instrument detail and performer posture
brief blackout or practical-light flare at section boundaries
lighting_language: practical backlights and sharp side keys reveal stage depth and equipment silhouettes
high contrast grows from isolated pools of light into wider chorus illumination
character_language: functional performance clothing with a strong readable silhouette and one song-specific detail
posture communicates effort, resistance, and release rather than fashion posing

acoustic_ballad.design_basis: Human-scale storytelling, tactile objects, lived-in rooms, landscape memory, and patient observation.
narrative_modes: move from a private everyday ritual toward a modest emotional opening
let one tactile object carry memory across changing places
use distance, weather, and domestic detail as emotional evidence
environments: small kitchen before sunrise with one chair pulled away from the table
quiet practice room with acoustic panels and an open instrument case
rural bus shelter beside a long road and changing weather
attic room with stored objects and soft window dust
modest community hall after everyone has left
camera_language: patient eye-level medium frame with meaningful empty space
close observation of hands, worn surfaces, and small object changes
slow doorway or window reveal that preserves natural spatial depth
long-lens landscape frame with restrained subject movement
motion_language: small breath, hand, curtain, steam, dust, and weather motion carry the rhythm
camera movement follows phrase length rather than every beat
emotional change appears through posture and distance before facial emphasis
edit_language: soft match dissolve through shape, gesture, or changing daylight
hold after the lyric ends before moving to the next location
cut between present detail and remembered space without literal flashback effects
lighting_language: motivated window, lamp, or overcast light with gentle falloff
climax expands available light while preserving an intimate scale
character_language: lived-in layered clothing with tactile fabric and quiet asymmetry
a familiar human silhouette defined by gesture and personal object rather than spectacle

hip_hop_trap.design_basis: Rhythm, self-authored identity, DJ and dance culture, neighborhood geometry, graphic surfaces, and social space.
narrative_modes: frame the lead as an author of the space rather than a passive figure in a dark street
build visual call-and-response between performer, crew, architecture, and beat
turn repeated routes, signs, steps, and circles into rhythmic structure
environments: community recreation room rearranged for a dance circle
record shop listening corner with layered sleeves and speaker stacks
rooftop practice area marked by painted floor geometry
daylight neighborhood court with graphic shadows and gathering space
compact DJ booth facing an open multipurpose floor
camera_language: grounded wide lens that keeps body movement and neighborhood geometry readable
lateral tracking synchronized to footwork and hand rhythm
circular composition around a dance or performance exchange
graphic top-down insert followed by an eye-level return
motion_language: footwork, shoulders, hands, fabric, turntables, and crowd response articulate the beat
camera accents follow bars and phrase changes instead of continuous shaking
foreground people and objects create layered rhythmic wipes
edit_language: cut on lyrical cadence with occasional held reaction frames
graphic match cut between circular, linear, or block-shaped forms
brief speed change reserved for a clear musical switch
lighting_language: practical street, shop, gym, or daylight sources create graphic contrast
color accents belong to clothing, murals, equipment, and architecture rather than generic neon
character_language: self-authored street styling with distinct layering, footwear silhouette, and personal accessories
confident stance and social awareness replace anonymous brooding

electronic_synth.design_basis: Time-based abstraction, light as a structural element, systems, interfaces, repetition, variation, and human-machine tension.
narrative_modes: introduce a simple visual rule, vary it, break it, then resolve it in a transformed system
let human gesture alter light, geometry, or interface behavior
use repetition with controlled deviation instead of random surreal imagery
environments: modular light chamber with movable translucent planes
analog control room with patch cables, meters, and geometric shadows
open black-box performance space defined by projected grids
kinetic installation corridor responding to footsteps
quiet observatory-like room with rotating optical elements
camera_language: precise symmetrical frame that slowly reveals one controlled deviation
measured orbit around layered translucent structures
macro interface detail expanding into architectural scale
locked geometric frame with motion occurring inside the composition
motion_language: loops evolve through phase shifts, parameter changes, and spatial layering
light, particles, panels, and reflections respond to musical structure
human motion remains simple so system behavior stays legible
edit_language: phase-aligned match cut between repeated geometric states
signal-like wipe or exposure change tied to a section transformation
long continuous build interrupted by one deliberate structural cut
lighting_language: light defines depth, rhythm, and interface state rather than decorating the scene
controlled gradients and optical reflections change according to musical layers
character_language: clean modular silhouette with one tactile imperfection or analog detail
gesture reads as operating, listening, or responding to a system

idol_pop.design_basis: Choreography, fashion-led identity, clear group geometry, cinematic set changes, and hook-centered visual recall.
narrative_modes: build each section around a clear visual hook that develops with the song
move between personal close-up storytelling and coordinated group release
use fashion, set transformation, and choreography as one coherent identity system
environments: transformable studio set with moving walls and visible depth changes
bright architectural courtyard designed for group formations
color-blocked transit concourse with strong vanishing lines
sunlit rehearsal loft that transforms for the chorus
graphic rooftop pavilion with layered platforms
camera_language: centered formation wide shot transitioning into a clean member close-up
crane or lift revealing choreography geometry without losing faces
smooth lateral travel through changing set layers
precise push-in timed to the hook gesture
motion_language: choreography shapes lines, diagonals, circles, and level changes
camera and performers exchange emphasis rather than moving at maximum intensity together
hair, fabric, props, and set pieces reinforce one readable hook
edit_language: match cut on a signature gesture between contrasting sets
clean beat cut between formation wide and emotional close-up
set transformation or lighting shift reserved for chorus lift
lighting_language: high-clarity key light protects faces while set lighting carries color and scale
chorus lighting broadens into layered depth rather than simple brightness
character_language: fashion silhouette is coordinated but each lead retains one distinct shape or accessory
expression, gesture, and styling communicate the song concept without copying a known group

jazz_soul.design_basis: Player-centered expression, ensemble dialogue, voice and instrument detail, social memory, and dynamic light across a shared space.
narrative_modes: treat musical exchange as conversation between faces, hands, breath, and room
move from solitary preparation toward ensemble or community connection
let repetition return with changed emotional weight
environments: small listening room with tables arranged around an intimate performance area
daytime rehearsal studio with sheet stands and worn wooden floor
community theater side stage with layered curtains and practical lamps
recording room separated by glass but visually connected
after-hours restaurant room rearranged for a live set
camera_language: medium two-shot that preserves eye line and musical exchange
close detail on breath, fingers, keys, strings, or microphone distance
slow pan across ensemble reactions before returning to the lead
layered frame through stands, curtains, or instrument silhouettes
motion_language: camera movement follows phrasing, call-and-response, and held notes
small performer reactions and ensemble timing carry emotional change
room atmosphere moves gently while hands and faces remain precise
edit_language: cut from lead phrase to listening reaction, then return on response
dissolve or pan transition motivated by a sustained note
hold a shared frame through the emotional peak instead of fragmenting it
lighting_language: layered practical light shapes faces, instruments, and depth without hiding expression
warm and cool zones distinguish solo focus from ensemble space
character_language: expressive but grounded clothing with material detail readable under practical light
identity is carried by posture, listening behavior, and relationship to instrument or voice
